question_text: Which of the following best explains why we see different phases of the Moon throughout the lunar cycle?

options.label: A
options.text: The Moon produces its own light, which varies in brightness each night

options.label: B
options.text: The amount of the Moon's sunlit side that we can see changes as the Moon orbits Earth

options.label: C
options.text: Earth's shadow gradually covers and uncovers the Moon each month

options.label: D
options.text: The Moon moves closer to and farther from Earth, making it appear brighter or dimmer

explanation: The Moon doesn't produce its own light—it reflects sunlight. As the Moon orbits Earth, different amounts of its illuminated surface face towards us, creating the phases we see. A full cycle takes about 28 days.
